pattern: Each vowel's follow rules define a fixed transition graph, so a length-by-length dynamic program that tallies strings ending in each vowel counts all valid strings in linear time under the modulus.
hints: Track, for each length, how many valid strings end in each of the five vowels.
A string ending in a given vowel can only be extended by the specific vowels that rule allows to follow it.
Read the rules backwards: the count ending in vowel x at length L is the sum of counts at length L-1 for every vowel that x is permitted to follow; sum the five totals at length n, all modulo 1e9+7.

edge_inputs.description: length one allows all five vowels
small length used in the statement
moderate length still well within exact integers before modulo
length large enough that the modulo actually wraps

stress.description: random lengths across the full range, checked against an independent matrix-power oracle
stress.note: stresses modular accumulation over long chains

solutions.python:
class Solution:
    def countVowelPermutation(self, n):
        MOD = 10**9 + 7
        a = e = i = o = u = 1
        for _ in range(n - 1):
            na = (e + i + u) % MOD
            ne = (a + i) % MOD
            ni = (e + o) % MOD
            no = i % MOD
            nu = (i + o) % MOD
            a, e, i, o, u = na, ne, ni, no, nu
        return (a + e + i + o + u) % MOD
